description: Golden identifier set the automated pipeline should retrieve/resolve for the diabetic-retinopathy autonomous-screening reference case. Companion to golden_expected_ids.json (HRV). Use in the ablation harness: score recall = golden ids present in the bundle's records (FDA codes EXACT; literature as a >=K resolvable-ID floor per COMPARISON.md Lesson 2), and precision = on-topic rate of what the pipeline returned. Every id below resolved live 2026-07-09 (PMIDs via E-utils, DOIs via Crossref; FDA codes + NCTs are verbatim openFDA / ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API responses).
reference_case: Autonomous AI detection of more-than-mild diabetic retinopathy (mtmDR) on non-mydriatic fundus photographs; point-of-care screening in primary care

match_policy.required_min_coverage: All ids in 'required' should be present for full recall. 'field_grounding' is the per-field breakdown. Unlike HRV (a regulatory NULL case), DR has named pivotal NCTs, so 'ncts' is a scored deterministic category here.
match_policy.resolution_pass: PMID -> E-utils esummary; DOI -> Crossref works; product_code -> openFDA classification; K/DEN -> openFDA 510k/De Novo; NCT -> ClinicalTrials.gov v2. A required id that fails to resolve is a TEST FAILURE, not a silent drop.
match_policy.regulatory_positive: Unlike the HRV null, openFDA MUST return an established device class here: product code PIB, 21 CFR 886.1100, Class II, classification-founding De Novo DEN180001, plus PIB-predicate 510(k)s. The PRESENCE of PIB is the Field-7 assertion.
match_policy.regulatory_null: No PMA (Class III) authorization exists for this device type (it is Class II). No standalone FDA-authorized image-quality-gate device exists distinct from the integrated screening systems.

2_input_signal_validation.note: Imageability/gradeability gate as a headline operating characteristic; camera qualification per model.

3_performance_benchmarks.note: No universal class-wide numeric standard. IDx-DR pivotal pre-specified endpoints (Se>85%, Sp>82.5%) are precedent only, not a codified threshold.

4_ground_truth.note: Independent masked reading-center reference (ETDRS severity scale + macular OCT for DME).

5_sample_size.note: Precedent enrollments (900 / 942 / 164 actual); exact N is study-defined.

6_subgroup.note: Skin tone / race-ethnicity, diabetes type, age, sex, and camera model. No retrieved source set a required per-skin-tone accuracy floor (gap).

7_regulatory.note: Class II, 21 CFR 886.1100; classification established by De Novo DEN180001; entrants clear via 510(k) against a PIB predicate.

8_monitoring.note: Real-world imageability/referral/follow-through surveillance; camera re-qualification. No mandated cadence retrieved (gap).

expected_conclusions.regulatory_class: U.S. FDA Class II, product code PIB, 21 CFR 886.1100 (Diabetic Retinopathy Detection Device); classification established by De Novo DEN180001; current entrants clear via 510(k) against a PIB predicate
expected_conclusions.benchmark_precedent_only: IDx-DR pivotal pre-specified Se>85% / Sp>82.5% (PMID 31304320); one trial's accepted endpoint, not a codified class-wide standard

coverage_gaps_expected: FDA De Novo/510(k) narrative decision-summary PDFs (accessdata.fda.gov; FDA-accepted acceptance criteria not readable via openFDA)
Verified per-skin-tone accuracy floor (none exists; flagged for expert review)
Post-market surveillance cadence / drift thresholds for this device class (none retrieved)
Drugs@FDA / drug labeling (not applicable; software diagnostic, not a drug/biologic)
